characters.name: open/close all folders
characters.description: Quintessential British Gentleman:The others regularly make jokes about how ridiculously posh he supposedly is.In an episode where Fry revealed he owned both a copper teakettle and an Aga stove, an exasperated Phill Jupitus exclaimed "It's no wonder Twinings had you, pal!"Many of Stephen's jokes work best if, like him, you attended a prestigious public school and an elite university. The others regularly make jokes about how ridiculously posh he supposedly is. In an episode where Fry revealed he owned both a copper teakettle and an Aga stove, an exasperated Phill Jupitus exclaimed "It's no wonder Twinings had you, pal!" Many of Stephen's jokes work best if, like him, you attended a prestigious public school and an elite university. Actor Allusion: Other panellists often make jabs at his role inJonathan Creek.

characters.name: Regulars
characters.description: Quintessential British Gentleman:The others regularly make jokes about how ridiculously posh he supposedly is.In an episode where Fry revealed he owned both a copper teakettle and an Aga stove, an exasperated Phill Jupitus exclaimed "It's no wonder Twinings had you, pal!"Many of Stephen's jokes work best if, like him, you attended a prestigious public school and an elite university. characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series A
characters.description: Renaissance Man: Comedian, actor, multi-instrumentalist, bird watcher, former butterfly collector, author... Fake Guest Star: Bill Bailey is a constanrt presence on QI, and has been in at least one episode of almost every series of the show, to the point of winning a certificate in Series U given to him by Sandi on behalf of production, for being the most common recurring guest star. Contractual Genre Blindness: On occasion. If she has already received a number of forfeits in an episode, she'll start trying to set the klaxon off deliberately. Dirty Old Woman: Makes the most dirty jokes of possibly anyone on the show. See "Jumble" for some particularly gross ones. Henpecked Husband: Has one, if she's to be believed.Played for Laughs.Of course, her real-life husband is much more attractive than she lets on, and by all accounts, they have a wonderful relationship.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series B
characters.description: Extreme Omnivore:Every time he's on he likes to tell people about his unusual appetite. Tortoise leg on toast anyone?He specifically noted once that he'd tried some puffin because he'd never had it before. Every time he's on he likes to tell people about his unusual appetite. Tortoise leg on toast anyone? He specifically noted once that he'd tried some puffin because he'd never had it before. Self-Deprecation: About Scotland. The Smart Guy: Science fan.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series C
characters.description: Troll: Discusses having been this in the Boy Scouts, when he was very young. Put on a Bus: Missed Series P, the only time he's done so since his debut. Unlike other panellists, however,The Bus Came Backas he returned as a frequent guest for Series Q. Rant Comedy: Many of his responses and reactions; especially prone to this if he gets the klaxon. TheBBCmade a supercut of his "angry logic". Sitcom Arch-Nemesis: The klaxon. The Smart Guy: One of the few who engages his brain when answering a question and not trying to show off.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series E
characters.description: Cloudcuckoolander: By far the panelist with the most out-there responses. The Ditz: Invoked; in theQImaking-of special, he has gone on record as trying to be dumber thanAlan. Once a Season: Consistently only appears on one episode every series (except for Series O, where he wasPut on a Bus).

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series G
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: He has managed to get an entire career out of doing this extremely well, after all. When He Smiles: It isverydifficult to get Jack to do this or burst out laughing on television (radio is different, but you can't see him in that case unless you go to the recording). If you have got this reaction, you have said something extremely funny. Brief Accent Imitation: Gets called out on it by Stephen Fry and David Tennant. Repeatedly. The Gadfly: He seems to have an ability to find out and push people'sBerserk Buttons. Contractual Genre Blindness: Shelovesgetting the klaxon, it seems.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series H
characters.description: Cloudcuckoolander: He has a tendency to bring the show on even wilder tangents than normal.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series J
characters.description: Butt-Monkey: Especially during Sandi's run, since she's one of the few people shorter than Sandi. Dreaming of Things to Come: Dreamed before the show that one of the questions would be "Why was the march hare important to the Aztecs". At the end of the show, this very question was asked. (Of course, it is likely the question was prepared during the show, however, it did in fact have a coherent answer, still fitting the spirit of this trope.) The Smart Guy: Has come in first or tied on the shows she's been on. Cool Teacher: Whenever he's gotten a question right, he always credits his childhood teacher, Mr Patel.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series K
characters.description: Funny Foreigner: Colin is Australian, and both he and the rest of the panel usually get some comedic mileage out of it. Unsportsmanlike Gloating: After his first win, he climbed onto the desk to celebrate. A Man Is Always Eager:Stephen: Who would bite their arm off to get their leg over?Josh:(buzzes in, silently points to himself)(klaxon) Suddenly Shouting: He screams instead of shouting, but he does this in reaction to Stephen smashing a rose dipped in liquid nitrogen on the desk.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series L
characters.description: Bile Fascination: Her reaction to Sandi confirming the purpose ofthe falcon sex hat(followed by a video demonstration) is either this, orCovert Pervert.She had the same facial expressions when Stephen explained the functioning of a male spider's pedipalps... Maybe it's both. She had the same facial expressions when Stephen explained the functioning of a male spider's pedipalps... Maybe it's both. Fan Disservice: Interprets Alan's "shark impression" as a badly-faked orgasm noise, and starts trying to one-up him. Obfuscating Stupidity: Puts on a ditzy persona when Sandi recruits her to help with a mathematical demonstration.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series M
characters.description: Serious Business: Do NOT say that two different types of Quality Street chocolate are the same as each other around him. Sustained Misunderstanding: How much sunlight does Denmark get, again? Distracted by the Sexy: A discussion of the origins of the Rorschach test ended quite abruptly when Cariad spied a picture of Dr. Rorschach and immediately caught the thirst. Awesome Ego: Apparently considers himself a narcissist and once bragged about the great reviews he got for playing Bottom inA Midsummer Night's Dream. However, it can be assumed he was at least partly joking on these occasions. Toilet Humor: After Holly Walsh mentioned that, under law, you can apparently post a gun to someone but not one with bullets in it, he joked that 'you can send a toilet, but not with a poo in it'.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series N
characters.description: Atomic F-Bomb: The loudest thing ever shouted wasnot"SHUT THE FUCK UP," but he was on the right track. Questionable Casting: Yes, that Corey Taylor. FromSlipknot. Yes, that Slipknot. (He fit insurprisingly well.)

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series O
characters.description: Shout-Out: When the topic of discussion in “Random” turned to coconuts, she anecdotally mentioned that they can’t be sent to Fiji via mail. Sandi was utterly confounded why Sally would try in the first place, until she mentioned she did it in theSeries 5 finaleofTaskmaster.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series P
characters.description: The Tease: Spent significant amounts of her appearance mercilessly flirting with Phil Jupitus.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series Q
characters.description: Accidental Truth: As Tom always appears in a smart suit, ChrisMcCauslandonce joked about the idea of a teenage Tom going to school in a suit with a pocket square. Tom then confirmed that heactually did that. Consistent Clothing Style: See above. He's always in a sharp suit and tie.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series R
characters.description: Deadpan Snarker: When Gyles Brandreth went on yet another tangent about a distant relation of his, she remarked that "It's not so much a family tree as a familyfuckingforest!". Cloud Cuckoolander: When Sandi asked the panel how to stop toes from falling off, Eshaan, for reasons beyond anyone else's comprehension, replied "Put them in the freezer." The Smart Guy: Comes with having a doctorate, really. At one point Ed Gamble remarked that being on a panel with her was like it was the morning of an exam, and Maggie was the only kid who'd actually done any revision.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series S
characters.description: The Smart Guy: Tends to be this.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series T
characters.description: The Smart Guy: Tends to be this.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series U
characters.description: Stating the Simple Solution: Usually offers up these sorts of answers in her time on the panel. In a quiz about which paintings are upside down, she points out that the ladder one of the museum staff is on top of is on top, so the painting must be hanging from the top too. Questionable Casting: She's not a well-known comedian outside New Zealand, so it's quite surprising that they were chosen as a panellist. That said, anyone familiar withTaskmaster New Zealandwill know of her.

characters.name: Panellists first appearing in series W
characters.description: Ascended Fanboy: A known fan of the show by his audience, he was able to appear in "Wings and Wheels". He even got to take home a few of the cards Sandi uses! The Smart Guy: While he doesn't get much time to shine, he certainly comes off as this regardless.
